samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed. 16-bit dividend. 8-bit divisor. 16-bit quotient. 16-bit remainder. The parameter i represents the current bit position of the divisor being compared with the dividend. Always block triggered by any change in the input values A and B. This block updates the values of the registers a_reg and b_reg with the values of A and B, respectively. These registers are used to store a copy of the input values, allowing them to be modified without affecting the original inputs.
Always block triggered by any change in the input values A and B. This block is used to perform the division operation. It compares the extracted bits of the dividend (a_reg) with the divisor (b_reg) for the current bit position (i). If the dividend bits are greater than or equal to the divisor, the quotient (result) is set to 1, and the extracted bits of the dividend are updated to the difference. If the dividend bits are less than the divisor, the quotient is set to 0, and the extracted bits of the dividend remain unchanged. The process is repeated for all 8 bits of the divisor, producing the final quotient and remainder.
